packageText:
# Diffuse Axonal Injury

Canonical hierarchy: All Categories > Basic > Neuroradiology > Brain > Primary Effects of CNS Trauma > Diffuse Axonal Injury
Canonical deck path: Corebook::Neuro::Brain::Primary Effects of CNS Trauma::Diffuse Axonal Injury
Primary source posture: RadPrimer supplies the canonical hierarchy/backbone; STATdx supplies supplemental depth and the default selected image set where it visually replaces RadPrimer duplicates.
Core evidence: no auditable Core Radiology source was supplied in this bundle, so Core-specific support is not asserted.

## Source Coverage And Image Gate

STATdx fully covers the 10-image RadPrimer teaching set with visually exact same-slice or same-graphic counterparts in the staged image_evidence files. The RadPrimer and STATdx binary hashes differ because the evidence exports have different dimensions/compression, but paired visual inspection of the actual files showed the same image for every mapped pair. Therefore the default teaching/download set uses STATdx images 1-41, while RadPrimer images 1-10 remain in the registry as archive-optional exact duplicates for audit traceability.

RadPrimer image 1 -> STATdx image 1: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 1 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 2 -> STATdx image 2: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 2 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 3 -> STATdx image 38: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 38 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 4 -> STATdx image 39: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 39 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 5 -> STATdx image 5: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 5 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 6 -> STATdx image 6: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 6 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 7 -> STATdx image 7: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 7 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 8 -> STATdx image 8: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 8 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 9 -> STATdx image 9: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 9 as the selected primary counterpart.
RadPrimer image 10 -> STATdx image 10: exact duplicate; use STATdx image 10 as the selected primary counterpart.
No RadPrimer image is classified as near duplicate, conceptual replacement, or not covered.

## Fused Teaching Backbone

### Definition And Mechanism

- [Both] Diffuse axonal injury is traumatic diffuse injury to axons in the cerebral hemispheres, corpus callosum, brainstem, and sometimes cerebellum, with concurrent capillary-level vascular injury.
- [Both] The mechanism is sudden acceleration, deceleration, or rotation with abrupt angular momentum change; direct head impact is not required.
- [Both] Axons are stretched more often than literally disconnected at the moment of trauma; later axonal swelling, impaired axoplasmic transport, cytotoxic edema, apoptosis, and Wallerian degeneration account for much of the injury.
- [Both] Injury concentrates where tissues of different density move differently, especially the gray-white junction, corpus callosum, deep gray nuclei/capsules, fornices, corona radiata, brainstem, and cerebellar peduncles.
- [Both] Traumatic microbleeds are an indirect imaging marker of axonal injury because they reflect associated small-vessel damage.

### Imaging Pattern

- [Both] Noncontrast CT is often normal in DAI, with normal initial CT reported in 50-80% of cases; about 75% of clinically suspected DAI with normal CT may have positive MR.
- [Both] CT-visible DAI appears as small hypodense nonhemorrhagic foci or small hyperdense hemorrhagic foci, often punctate to 15 mm and often multiple/bilateral.
- [Both] Typical locations are the frontotemporal gray-white interface, subcortical/deep white matter, corpus callosum, deep gray nuclei, internal/external capsules, fornices, corona radiata, dorsolateral midbrain, and upper pons.
- [Both] Deeper involvement means more severe injury and poorer prognosis; dorsolateral midbrain/upper pontine involvement is especially ominous.
- [Both] FLAIR is key for nonhemorrhagic hyperintense lesions; T2* GRE shows hemorrhagic blooming; SWI depicts substantially more susceptibility foci than GRE; DWI can show restricted diffusion.
- [Both] DTI/tractography can show white-matter tract disruption even when routine MR and GRE are normal; MRS may show reduced NAA/Cr and increased Cho/Cr in normal-appearing or visibly injured brain.
- [RadPrimer] RadPrimer stresses that absent tSAH makes severe DAI unlikely and absent intraventricular hemorrhage makes any DAI less likely.
- [STATdx] STATdx phrases this more cautiously: midline tSAH may suggest DAI, particularly when intraventricular hemorrhage is present.

### Staging And Prognosis

- [Both] Adams/Gennarelli stage 1 involves frontal and temporal gray-white interface lesions; stage 2 adds lobar white matter and corpus callosum; stage 3 includes dorsolateral midbrain and upper pons.
- [STATdx] Extended grading language adds that substantia nigra or mesencephalic tegmentum involvement may further worsen prognosis.
- [Both] Eighty percent of lesions are microscopic and nonhemorrhagic, so visible lesions are the tip of the iceberg.
- [Both] Prognosis worsens with more lesions and deeper lesions; brainstem injury is associated with immediate or early death.

### Differential Diagnosis

- [Both] Multifocal nonhemorrhagic mimics include aging-related leukoaraiosis/lacunes and demyelinating disease.
- [Both] Multifocal hemorrhagic mimics include cerebral amyloid angiopathy, chronic hypertension, cavernous malformations, hemorrhagic tumors, and acute hemorrhagic leukoencephalopathy.
- [Both] Clinical trauma context, lesion distribution at shear interfaces, corpus callosum/brainstem involvement, and the mismatch between poor neurologic status and subtle CT help favor DAI.

### Clinical Context And Management

- [Both] DAI is usually associated with high-velocity motor vehicle trauma but can occur after low-energy trauma such as stair falls or ground-level falls.
- [Both] Suspect DAI when GCS or neurologic impairment is disproportionate to CT findings.
- [Both] Clinical DAI is prolonged posttraumatic coma of at least 6 hours without a mass lesion explaining the coma; severity is graded by coma duration and brainstem deficit.
- [Both] DAI is the most common primary traumatic neuronal injury in the supplied source text and is a major cause of death/disability in children and young adults.
- [Both] There is no lesion-specific curative treatment; management is supportive and treats comorbid herniation, hemorrhage, hydrocephalus, and seizures.

### Diagnostic Checklist

- [Both] In trauma with low GCS and minimal CT findings, obtain MR with FLAIR, DWI, and hemorrhage-sensitive imaging.
- [Both] Use SWI when available for hemorrhagic DAI; use T2* GRE if SWI is unavailable.
- [Both] Search the gray-white junction, corpus callosum (especially splenium/posterior body), deep gray nuclei/capsules, fornices, corona radiata, dorsolateral midbrain, upper pons, and cerebellar peduncles.
- [Both] Report lesion burden, hemorrhagic versus nonhemorrhagic pattern, callosal/brainstem involvement, intraventricular hemorrhage, tSAH, mass lesions, hydrocephalus, and associated traumatic injuries.

## Selected Image Atlas For Future Generation

Use the following primary images by default for download and teaching. RadPrimer images 1-10 are retained only as archive-optional duplicate evidence because STATdx provides visually exact higher-resolution counterparts.

- STATdx image 1: common-site/severity graphic; replaces RadPrimer image 1.
- STATdx image 2: autopsy hemorrhagic DAI; replaces RadPrimer image 2.
- STATdx images 3 and 4: atomic CT/SWI high-speed MVA cluster with SAH and gray-white interface microhemorrhages.
- STATdx image 5: sagittal corpus callosum/brainstem graphic; replaces RadPrimer image 5.
- STATdx images 6-10: atomic low-GCS/near-normal CT to MR cluster; STATdx images 6-10 replace RadPrimer images 6-10 respectively.
- STATdx images 11-15: atomic torn-arachnoid/IVH/subdural-hygroma/severe diffuse vascular injury cluster.
- STATdx images 16-20: FLAIR, GRE, SWI MIP, cephalad SWI, and DWI cluster showing why SWI/DWI expand detection.
- STATdx image 21: microscopic postmortem retraction-ball pathology.
- STATdx images 22-26: focal bifrontal, callosal/splenial, fornix, frontal subcortical, and mesencephalic examples.
- STATdx images 27-29: atomic normal conventional CT/T2 then GRE-detected frontal WM and cerebellar peduncle microbleed cluster.
- STATdx images 30-34: additional GRE/CT/DWI examples for gray-white, pericallosal, fornix, and splenial involvement.
- STATdx images 35-37: atomic motorcycle GCS 3 midbrain FLAIR/DWI/SWI cluster.
- STATdx images 38 and 39: higher-resolution exact CT counterparts for RadPrimer images 3 and 4.
- STATdx images 40 and 41: dorsolateral midbrain hemorrhage and evolving brainstem DAI examples.

## Downstream Use Notes

- Use `selectedPrimaryImageIds` for default image download and teaching.
- Use `archiveOptionalImageIds` only for exact duplicate audit recovery; do not download them by default.
- Preserve same-patient, follow-up, multimodality, pathology, and adjacent-slice clusters as atomic unless a later source explicitly documents a safe split.
- Keep human-facing prose labels clean, such as RadPrimer image 5 or STATdx image 4. Use short IDs such as RP-05 and SDX-04 only in registry, filenames, and traceability fields.

Keep RP-05 and SDX-04 style IDs in registry, filenames, manifest, and traceability fields.

sourceSelectionPlan.imageCurationPolicy: Exact duplicates may be archived; near duplicates and conceptual replacements remain selected as recognition reinforcement unless visually identical or unusable. Download only selectedPrimaryImageIds by default.
sourceSelectionPlan.duplicateEvidencePolicy: Exact duplicate decisions in this package are based on actual image_evidence file inspection showing the same slice/screenshot/graphic. Caption-only or topic-only similarity was not used for exact duplicate status.

imageDownloadPlan.teachingRole: Mechanism/topography graphic: common DAI sites and severity gradient with deeper involvement.
Autopsy hemorrhagic DAI pattern in frontal white matter, basal ganglia, external capsule, and gray-white junction.
Trauma CT with sylvian SAH and inferior frontal hemorrhage, an early CT clue cluster.
Same-patient SWI/MIP microhemorrhage at gray-white interfaces after decreased GCS.
Sagittal graphic of corpus callosum and brainstem hemorrhagic foci.
Severe clinical DAI with near-normal CT/diffuse swelling and minimal tSAH.
Same-patient FLAIR corpus callosum and subcortical white-matter edema after discrepant CT/GCS.
Same-patient SWI MIP showing innumerable axonal-course microhemorrhages in corona radiata.
Same-patient DWI restricted diffusion in frontal white matter, corpus callosum, fornices, and subcortical white matter.
Same-patient sagittal DWI showing callosal and midbrain restricted diffusion.
T2 MR thalamic and bifrontal white-matter hyperintensities with torn-arachnoid/intraventricular blood context.
Same-patient SWI severe diffuse vascular injury with innumerable deep/subcortical blooming lesions.
High-impact CT with IVH, subdural hygromas, torn arachnoid, and minimal tSAH.
Same-patient cephalad CT with tSAH and falcine trace subdural hemorrhage.
Same-patient T2 MR subdural hygromas, preserving the torn-arachnoid cluster.
FLAIR subcortical/deep white-matter hyperintensities with tSAH.
GRE linear hemorrhages in subcortical white matter and corpus callosum.
SWI MIP showing many more lesions than GRE across WM, basal ganglia, corpus callosum, and fornices.
Cephalad SWI showing end-on dot-like susceptibility foci.
DWI restricted diffusion in fornices and scattered subcortical/deep white matter.
Microscopic postmortem axonal retraction balls and parenchymal holes.
Bifrontal GRE susceptibility foci as hemorrhagic DAI.
FLAIR callosal edema with central hemorrhage plus parietal subcortical edema.
NECT splenial callosal hemorrhage with intraventricular spread.
Same-patient NECT additional frontal subcortical white-matter hemorrhage.
GRE posterolateral mesencephalic hemorrhage plus temporal/frontal microbleeds.
Normal T2 MR despite severe posttraumatic symptoms, reinforcing occult DAI limits.
Same-patient GRE frontal white-matter microbleeds despite normal CT/T2.
Same-patient GRE left superior cerebellar peduncle hemorrhage.
GRE multifocal gray-white and periventricular susceptibility foci.
GRE multifocal deep white-matter/pericallosal susceptibility foci.
NECT bifrontal gray-white petechial hemorrhages with SAH.
NECT fornix hemorrhage and nonhemorrhagic splenial involvement.
DWI restricted diffusion/susceptibility in fornices and splenium.
Motorcycle GCS 3 FLAIR posterior midbrain edema/hemorrhage.
Same-patient DWI posterior midbrain cytotoxic edema.
Same-patient SWI midbrain hemorrhage with scattered SAH.
Higher-resolution counterpart for RadPrimer image 3: CT hemorrhagic DAI with IVH.
Higher-resolution counterpart for RadPrimer image 4: cephalad CT punctate peripheral hemorrhages/tSAH.
NECT dorsolateral midbrain hemorrhage with interpeduncular cisternal SAH.
T2 evolving dorsolateral brainstem DAI with chronic bitemporal subdural hematomas.

caseClusterGuardrails.policy: Same-patient, follow-up, procedure, adjacent-slice, time-lapse, and multimodality clusters are atomic unless an explicit source-supported split is safe.

atomicClusters.reason: same-patient CT/SWI pair after high-speed MVA and decreased GCS
same patient with low GCS, subtle CT, then FLAIR/SWI/DWI evidence
same-patient CT/MR cluster with IVH, tSAH, subdural hygromas, and severe diffuse vascular injury
multisequence FLAIR/GRE/SWI/DWI depiction of WM hemorrhage and restricted diffusion
same-patient normal CT/T2 followed by GRE-detected microbleeds and cerebellar peduncle hemorrhage
same-patient FLAIR, DWI, and SWI midbrain injury after motorcycle accident
STATdx higher-resolution same-slice counterparts for the RadPrimer CT pair

images.caption: Graphics depict the most common sites of axonal injury (red). Frequent but relatively less common locations are shown (green). Injury to midbrain/upper pons (purple) is uncommon but often lethal. In general, the deeper the abnormalities, the more severe the injury.
images.cleanCaption: Graphics depict the most common sites of axonal injury (red). Frequent but relatively less common locations are shown (green). Injury to midbrain/upper pons (purple) is uncommon but often lethal. In general, the deeper the abnormalities, the more severe the injury.

images.classificationRationale: Actual image_evidence visual inspection showed the same slice/screenshot/graphic as the mapped STATdx image; archived because the STATdx counterpart is selected for default download.

images.caption: Autopsy shows hemorrhagic diffuse axonal injury (DAI). Note the linear hemorrhagic foci in the frontal lobe WM as well as the basal ganglia and external capsule . There is a hemorrhagic lesion at the junction of the frontal lobe cortex and subcortical WM .

images.caption: Axial SWI MR in the same patient with MIP shows innumerable punctate and linear, hypointense foci that follow the course of the axons within the corona radiata. DAI is best detected by FLAIR for nonhemorrhagic and SWI for hemorrhagic lesions.
images.cleanCaption: Axial SWI MR in the same patient with MIP shows innumerable punctate and linear, hypointense foci that follow the course of the axons within the corona radiata. DAI is best detected by FLAIR for nonhemorrhagic and SWI for hemorrhagic lesions.
images.teachingRole: Same-patient SWI MIP showing innumerable axonal-course microhemorrhages in corona radiata.
images.classificationRationale: Selected higher-resolution STATdx counterpart after actual image_evidence visual inspection confirmed it is the same slice/screenshot/graphic as the mapped RadPrimer image.

images.caption: Axial DWI MR in the same patient shows intense restriction in the frontal WM as well as throughout the corpus callosum , both fornices , and subcortical WM .
images.cleanCaption: Axial DWI MR in the same patient shows intense restriction in the frontal WM as well as throughout the corpus callosum , both fornices , and subcortical WM .
images.teachingRole: Same-patient DWI restricted diffusion in frontal white matter, corpus callosum, fornices, and subcortical white matter.
images.classificationRationale: Selected higher-resolution STATdx counterpart after actual image_evidence visual inspection confirmed it is the same slice/screenshot/graphic as the mapped RadPrimer image.

images.caption: Midline sagittal DWI MR in the same patient shows the intense restricted diffusion throughout the corpus callosum . A smaller focus of restricted diffusion is present in the midbrain .
images.cleanCaption: Midline sagittal DWI MR in the same patient shows the intense restricted diffusion throughout the corpus callosum . A smaller focus of restricted diffusion is present in the midbrain .
images.teachingRole: Same-patient sagittal DWI showing callosal and midbrain restricted diffusion.
images.classificationRationale: Selected higher-resolution STATdx counterpart after actual image_evidence visual inspection confirmed it is the same slice/screenshot/graphic as the mapped RadPrimer image.

images.caption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient (obtained immediately following the CT because of the gross discrepancy between clinical and imaging findings) shows hyperintensities in the right thalamus and WM of both frontal lobes . A torn arachnoid is probably responsible for the small bifrontal subdural hygromas .
images.cleanCaption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient (obtained immediately following the CT because of the gross discrepancy between clinical and imaging findings) shows hyperintensities in the right thalamus and WM of both frontal lobes . A torn arachnoid is probably responsible for the small bifrontal subdural hygromas .
images.teachingRole: T2 MR thalamic and bifrontal white-matter hyperintensities with torn-arachnoid/intraventricular blood context.
images.classificationRationale: Selected as supplemental recognition reinforcement; not an exact RadPrimer duplicate and not unusable on visual review.

images.caption: Axial SWI MR in the same patient shows innumerable linear and ovoid blooming hypointensities in the subcortical and deep WM , consistent with diffuse vascular injury (severe DAI).
images.cleanCaption: Axial SWI MR in the same patient shows innumerable linear and ovoid blooming hypointensities in the subcortical and deep WM , consistent with diffuse vascular injury (severe DAI).
images.teachingRole: Same-patient SWI severe diffuse vascular injury with innumerable deep/subcortical blooming lesions.
images.classificationRationale: Selected as supplemental recognition reinforcement; not an exact RadPrimer duplicate and not unusable on visual review.

images.caption: Axial NECT in a patient who was in a high-impact MVA with GCS = 5 shows some intraventricular hemorrhage and subdural hygromas , probably a torn arachnoid. Minimal tSAH is present.
images.cleanCaption: Axial NECT in a patient who was in a high-impact MVA with GCS = 5 shows some intraventricular hemorrhage and subdural hygromas , probably a torn arachnoid. Minimal tSAH is present.
images.teachingRole: High-impact CT with IVH, subdural hygromas, torn arachnoid, and minimal tSAH.
images.classificationRationale: Selected as supplemental recognition reinforcement; not an exact RadPrimer duplicate and not unusable on visual review.

images.caption: More cephalad NECT in the same patient shows tSAH and trace subdural hemorrhage along the falx .
images.cleanCaption: More cephalad NECT in the same patient shows tSAH and trace subdural hemorrhage along the falx .
images.teachingRole: Same-patient cephalad CT with tSAH and falcine trace subdural hemorrhage.
images.classificationRationale: Selected as supplemental recognition reinforcement; not an exact RadPrimer duplicate and not unusable on visual review.

images.caption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient shows the subdural hygromas .
images.cleanCaption: Axial T2 MR in the same patient shows the subdural hygromas .
images.teachingRole: Same-patient T2 MR subdural hygromas, preserving the torn-arachnoid cluster.
images.classificationRationale: Selected as supplemental recognition reinforcement; not an exact RadPrimer duplicate and not unusable on visual review.

images.caption: Axial FLAIR MR shows some hyperintensities in the subcortical and deep WM along with tSAH .
images.cleanCaption: Axial FLAIR MR shows some hyperintensities in the subcortical and deep WM along with tSAH .
images.teachingRole: FLAIR subcortical/deep white-matter hyperintensities with tSAH.

images.caption: Axial T2* GRE MR shows linear hemorrhages in the subcortical WM and corpus callosum .

images.teachingRole: GRE linear hemorrhages in subcortical white matter and corpus callosum.
images.classificationRationale: Selected as supplemental recognition reinforcement; not an exact RadPrimer duplicate and not unusable on visual review.

images.caption: Axial T2* SWI MR with MIP shows many more linear and punctate hemorrhages in the subcortical and deep periventricular WM as well as in the basal ganglia , corpus callosum , and fornices .
images.cleanCaption: Axial T2* SWI MR with MIP shows many more linear and punctate hemorrhages in the subcortical and deep periventricular WM as well as in the basal ganglia , corpus callosum , and fornices .

images.caption: More cephalad T2* SWI MR shows the WM hemorrhages "end on" as multifocal dot-like foci of susceptibility .

images.caption: Axial DWI MR shows restricted diffusion in the fornices as well as scattered foci in the subcortical and deep WM.

images.caption: Microscopic postmortem study of WM in severe DAI shows axonal "retraction balls" and numerous empty "holes" in the brain parenchyma.

imageDownloadPlan.teachingRole: Mechanism/topography graphic: common DAI sites and severity gradient with deeper involvement.
Autopsy hemorrhagic DAI pattern in frontal white matter, basal ganglia, external capsule, and gray-white junction.
Trauma CT with sylvian SAH and inferior frontal hemorrhage, an early CT clue cluster.
Same-patient SWI/MIP microhemorrhage at gray-white interfaces after decreased GCS.
Sagittal graphic of corpus callosum and brainstem hemorrhagic foci.
Severe clinical DAI with near-normal CT/diffuse swelling and minimal tSAH.
Same-patient FLAIR corpus callosum and subcortical white-matter edema after discrepant CT/GCS.
Same-patient SWI MIP showing innumerable axonal-course microhemorrhages in corona radiata.
Same-patient DWI restricted diffusion in frontal white matter, corpus callosum, fornices, and subcortical white matter.
Same-patient sagittal DWI showing callosal and midbrain restricted diffusion.
T2 MR thalamic and bifrontal white-matter hyperintensities with torn-arachnoid/intraventricular blood context.
Same-patient SWI severe diffuse vascular injury with innumerable deep/subcortical blooming lesions.
High-impact CT with IVH, subdural hygromas, torn arachnoid, and minimal tSAH.
Same-patient cephalad CT with tSAH and falcine trace subdural hemorrhage.
Same-patient T2 MR subdural hygromas, preserving the torn-arachnoid cluster.
FLAIR subcortical/deep white-matter hyperintensities with tSAH.
GRE linear hemorrhages in subcortical white matter and corpus callosum.
SWI MIP showing many more lesions than GRE across WM, basal ganglia, corpus callosum, and fornices.
Cephalad SWI showing end-on dot-like susceptibility foci.
DWI restricted diffusion in fornices and scattered subcortical/deep white matter.
Microscopic postmortem axonal retraction balls and parenchymal holes.
Bifrontal GRE susceptibility foci as hemorrhagic DAI.
FLAIR callosal edema with central hemorrhage plus parietal subcortical edema.
NECT splenial callosal hemorrhage with intraventricular spread.
Same-patient NECT additional frontal subcortical white-matter hemorrhage.
GRE posterolateral mesencephalic hemorrhage plus temporal/frontal microbleeds.
Normal T2 MR despite severe posttraumatic symptoms, reinforcing occult DAI limits.
Same-patient GRE frontal white-matter microbleeds despite normal CT/T2.
Same-patient GRE left superior cerebellar peduncle hemorrhage.
GRE multifocal gray-white and periventricular susceptibility foci.
GRE multifocal deep white-matter/pericallosal susceptibility foci.
NECT bifrontal gray-white petechial hemorrhages with SAH.
NECT fornix hemorrhage and nonhemorrhagic splenial involvement.
DWI restricted diffusion/susceptibility in fornices and splenium.
Motorcycle GCS 3 FLAIR posterior midbrain edema/hemorrhage.
Same-patient DWI posterior midbrain cytotoxic edema.
Same-patient SWI midbrain hemorrhage with scattered SAH.
Higher-resolution counterpart for RadPrimer image 3: CT hemorrhagic DAI with IVH.
Higher-resolution counterpart for RadPrimer image 4: cephalad CT punctate peripheral hemorrhages/tSAH.
NECT dorsolateral midbrain hemorrhage with interpeduncular cisternal SAH.
T2 evolving dorsolateral brainstem DAI with chronic bitemporal subdural hematomas.

sourcePriority: STATdx supplemental depth and selected primary images where visually superior or duplicate-replacing

keepFromRadPrimer: core article structure: terminology, imaging, differential, pathology, clinical issues, and diagnostic checklist
RadPrimer image numbering traceability even when archived
keepFromSTATdx: higher-resolution counterparts for the full RadPrimer image set
additional CT, FLAIR, GRE/SWI, DWI, pathology, normal-conventional-MR, and brainstem examples
updated wording on midline tSAH with IVH, SWI greater than GRE, MRS outcome markers, and extended midbrain prognostic grading
modality-specific image atlas captions and same-patient clusters
downweightOrSkip: STATdx breadcrumb routing for manifest or deck path
Core Radiology claims without supplied auditable Core source
caption-only duplicate claims without image_evidence support
IMAIOS label repository noise except for preserving the canonical deck breadcrumb from metadata.json

sourceSelectionPlan.imageCurationPolicy: Exact duplicates may be archived; near duplicates and conceptual replacements remain selected as recognition reinforcement unless visually identical or unusable. Download only selectedPrimaryImageIds by default.
sourceSelectionPlan.duplicateEvidencePolicy: Exact duplicate decisions in this package are based on actual image_evidence file inspection showing the same slice/screenshot/graphic. Caption-only or topic-only similarity was not used for exact duplicate status.

caseClusterGuardrails.policy: Same-patient, follow-up, procedure, adjacent-slice, time-lapse, and multimodality clusters are atomic unless an explicit source-supported split is safe.

generatorInstructions: Do not generate cards or lecture from this handoff alone; use it only as the fused source package for later runs.
Use source-qualified prose labels such as RadPrimer image 5 or STATdx image 4; keep RP-05 and SDX-04 in traceability fields only.
Use selectedPrimaryImageIds for default image download and teaching; archiveOptionalImageIds are retained for audit/completeness only.
Preserve source attribution with [RadPrimer], [STATdx], and [Both] where useful.
